verses.text: At Antioch the Spirit says, 'Separate me Barnabas and Saul'; they lay hands and send them. Sent by God. The first missionary journey begins at the Spirit's call.
At Antioch the Spirit says, 'Separate me Barnabas and Saul'; they lay hands and send them. Sent by God. The first missionary journey begins at the Spirit's call.
At Antioch the Spirit says, 'Separate me Barnabas and Saul'; they lay hands and send them. Sent by God. The first missionary journey begins at the Spirit's call.
They preach in Cyprus in the synagogues, with John attending. On the way. The gospel goes out from town to town.
They preach in Cyprus in the synagogues, with John attending. On the way. The gospel goes out from town to town.
Elymas the sorcerer withstands them, seeking to turn the deputy from the faith. Opposition. The enemy resists the spreading word.
Elymas the sorcerer withstands them, seeking to turn the deputy from the faith. Opposition. The enemy resists the spreading word.
Elymas the sorcerer withstands them, seeking to turn the deputy from the faith. Opposition. The enemy resists the spreading word.
Paul strikes him blind; the deputy believes, 'astonished at the doctrine.' Power and faith. God overrules the foe and wins the ruler.
Paul strikes him blind; the deputy believes, 'astonished at the doctrine.' Power and faith. God overrules the foe and wins the ruler.
Paul strikes him blind; the deputy believes, 'astonished at the doctrine.' Power and faith. God overrules the foe and wins the ruler.
Paul strikes him blind; the deputy believes, 'astonished at the doctrine.' Power and faith. God overrules the foe and wins the ruler.
At Antioch in Pisidia they are asked for 'a word of exhortation.' An open door. The synagogue invites the messengers to speak.
At Antioch in Pisidia they are asked for 'a word of exhortation.' An open door. The synagogue invites the messengers to speak.
At Antioch in Pisidia they are asked for 'a word of exhortation.' An open door. The synagogue invites the messengers to speak.
Paul rehearses the exodus and God's care in the wilderness. From history. He grounds the gospel in God's dealings with Israel.
Paul rehearses the exodus and God's care in the wilderness. From history. He grounds the gospel in God's dealings with Israel.
Paul rehearses the exodus and God's care in the wilderness. From history. He grounds the gospel in God's dealings with Israel.
Paul rehearses the exodus and God's care in the wilderness. From history. He grounds the gospel in God's dealings with Israel.
He tells of the judges and David, 'a man after mine own heart.' To David's line. The story leads toward the promised Son.
He tells of the judges and David, 'a man after mine own heart.' To David's line. The story leads toward the promised Son.
He tells of the judges and David, 'a man after mine own heart.' To David's line. The story leads toward the promised Son.
Of David's seed God raised 'a Saviour, Jesus'; John, unworthy to loose His shoe, went before. The Saviour come. The promise is fulfilled in Christ.
Of David's seed God raised 'a Saviour, Jesus'; John, unworthy to loose His shoe, went before. The Saviour come. The promise is fulfilled in Christ.
Of David's seed God raised 'a Saviour, Jesus'; John, unworthy to loose His shoe, went before. The Saviour come. The promise is fulfilled in Christ.
To you is the word of salvation sent; those at Jerusalem, condemning Him, fulfilled the prophets. Unwitting fulfilment. Their crime accomplished God's word.
To you is the word of salvation sent; those at Jerusalem, condemning Him, fulfilled the prophets. Unwitting fulfilment. Their crime accomplished God's word.
To you is the word of salvation sent; those at Jerusalem, condemning Him, fulfilled the prophets. Unwitting fulfilment. Their crime accomplished God's word.
To you is the word of salvation sent; those at Jerusalem, condemning Him, fulfilled the prophets. Unwitting fulfilment. Their crime accomplished God's word.
'But God raised him from the dead'; He was seen many days; the promise is fulfilled. Resurrection. The risen Christ is the heart of the gospel.
'But God raised him from the dead'; He was seen many days; the promise is fulfilled. Resurrection. The risen Christ is the heart of the gospel.
'But God raised him from the dead'; He was seen many days; the promise is fulfilled. Resurrection. The risen Christ is the heart of the gospel.
'But God raised him from the dead'; He was seen many days; the promise is fulfilled. Resurrection. The risen Christ is the heart of the gospel.
He whom God raised 'saw no corruption'; David died and saw it, but this man did not. Greater than David. The deathless Christ outshines the prophet-king.
He whom God raised 'saw no corruption'; David died and saw it, but this man did not. Greater than David. The deathless Christ outshines the prophet-king.
He whom God raised 'saw no corruption'; David died and saw it, but this man did not. Greater than David. The deathless Christ outshines the prophet-king.
He whom God raised 'saw no corruption'; David died and saw it, but this man did not. Greater than David. The deathless Christ outshines the prophet-king.
Through this man is preached forgiveness; 'by him all that believe are justified from all things.' Free justification. Faith in Christ does what the law could not.
Through this man is preached forgiveness; 'by him all that believe are justified from all things.' Free justification. Faith in Christ does what the law could not.
Beware, lest the prophets' word fall, 'Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish.' A warning. To despise the gospel is to court ruin.
Beware, lest the prophets' word fall, 'Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish.' A warning. To despise the gospel is to court ruin.
The Gentiles beg the word be preached again; many follow Paul and Barnabas. Eager hearers. The grace of God draws them on.
The Gentiles beg the word be preached again; many follow Paul and Barnabas. Eager hearers. The grace of God draws them on.
The envious Jews contradict; 'lo, we turn to the Gentiles,' a light to the ends of the earth. Door opened. Rejection by some sends the gospel to others.
The envious Jews contradict; 'lo, we turn to the Gentiles,' a light to the ends of the earth. Door opened. Rejection by some sends the gospel to others.
The envious Jews contradict; 'lo, we turn to the Gentiles,' a light to the ends of the earth. Door opened. Rejection by some sends the gospel to others.
The envious Jews contradict; 'lo, we turn to the Gentiles,' a light to the ends of the earth. Door opened. Rejection by some sends the gospel to others.
The Gentiles are glad; 'as many as were ordained to eternal life believed'; the word spreads. Sovereign grace. Those appointed to life receive it gladly.
The Gentiles are glad; 'as many as were ordained to eternal life believed'; the word spreads. Sovereign grace. Those appointed to life receive it gladly.
Persecuted, they shake off the dust and go to Iconium, 'filled with joy.' Joy in trial. Opposition cannot dim their gladness.
Persecuted, they shake off the dust and go to Iconium, 'filled with joy.' Joy in trial. Opposition cannot dim their gladness.
Persecuted, they shake off the dust and go to Iconium, 'filled with joy.' Joy in trial. Opposition cannot dim their gladness.
